homepage.title: Will Stone MP
homepage.text_excerpt: Will Stone MP Home News Newsletter Pride in Penhill Survey Get In Touch How I got here I was born and raised in Swindon. I grew up in the Pinehurst area and went to Headlands School. After leaving school, I worked various jobs from factories to call centres, but it wasn’t for me so I decided to take a leap and join the army in the 1st Battalion the rifles. I absolutely loved my time in the army but after 5 years I decided to leave to set up a small business in Swindon. It was at this point that I got involved in politics and joined the Labour Party. After being heavily involved in local campaigning, I stood for parish council and then borough, winning both seats. Then I put my hat in the ring to stand as Member of Parliament for Swindon North. Why? Because I’d seen too many politicians get into power and do nothing. I’m proud to be Swindon North’s first MP to be born in Swindon and deliver for the town, I’m bringing manufacturing jobs back to Swindon through turning the town into the UK’s Drone Hub. So far we’ve delivered over 800 jobs and I’m committed to delivering more. But I won’t stop there. My missions for our town are simple to build a more prosperous more secure place. Create Jobs & Investment Bring more Education Opportunities through apprenticeships and college courses Build Community and provide better mental health support I’m bringing manufacturing back to our town, including jobs at: Pinetrees Community Centre, The Circle, Swindon, SN2 1RF 01793 540040 will@willstone.org.uk Links Standards Policy Privacy Policy Socials Facebook LinkedIn Instagram Bluesky YouTube TikTok © 2025 Will Stone MP

headings: h2: Will Stone’s Amendments included in Government’s Road Safety Strategy Consultation – Running until 31st March 2026

h2: “I believe in leading from the front. That’s why I’m joining up to the reserves. In such turbulent times, it’s on all of us to step up and do our part. Through serving as your MP, working with the Drone Sector to bring jobs and joining up I plan on doing my part.”
h2: Will Stone Year In Review
pages.text_excerpt: News – Will Stone MP Home News Newsletter Pride in Penhill Survey Get In Touch News 7th January 2026 – Pinned Will Stone’s Amendments included in Government’s Road Safety Strategy Consultation – Running until 31st March 2026 Take part in the consultation and help get Harry Parker’s Amendment’s into Government Policy Will is bringing forward the following amendments to the Road Safety Consultation: “Failure to stop 1. The Road Traffic Act 1988 is amended as follows. 2. In Section 170, after subsection (4) insert— (4A) The maximum penalties available to the Courts when sentencing an individual who has been convicted of an offence under this section are as follows— (a) An unlimited fine; (b) A custodial sentence of one year; and (c) Disqualification from driving for a period of up to two years When considering its sentence, the Court may issue more than one of the maximum penalties listed above.” “Unlicensed drivers: penalties 1. The Road Traffic Act 1988 is amended as follows. 2. In Section 87, after subsection (2) insert— (2A) The maximum penalty available to the Courts when sentencing an individual who has been convicted of driving without a license, and who has never held a license, shall be an unlimited fine, or a custodial sentence of six months (or both). Take part in the Consultation 12th January 2026 Will Stone returning to the Army, committed to joining the Army Reserves “I believe in leading from the front. That’s why I’m joining up to the reserves. In such turbulent times, it’s on all of us to step up and do our part. Through serving as your MP, working with the Drone Sector to bring jobs and joining up I plan on doing my part.” 31st December 2025 Will Stone Year In Review This Year Will Stone brought the first stages of his plan to deindustrialise Swindon into fruition, with over 800 jobs brought to the town from various Drone manufacturing partners like STARK, TEKEVER and FLYBY Dynamics. He also supported a number of residents through local constituency work. Pinetrees Community Centre, The Circle, Swindon, SN2 1RF 01793 540040 will@willstone.org.uk Links Standards Policy Privacy Policy Socials Facebook LinkedIn Instagram Bluesky YouTube TikTok © 2025 Will Stone MP

pages.title: Pride in Penhill Survey – Will Stone MP
pages.text_excerpt: Pride in Penhill Survey – Will Stone MP Home News Newsletter Pride in Penhill Survey Get In Touch Pride in Penhill Survey Please enable JavaScript in your browser to complete this form. Please enable JavaScript in your browser to complete this form. This questionnaire is to make sure that the Pride in Place Programme reflects what you, the residents of Penhill want from the £20m. As your MP I am committed to making sure your voice gets heard and the community, not politicians make the decisions. Name * First Last Address * Address Line 1 Address Line 2 City State / Province / Region Postal Code Email * What do you enjoy most about living here? Which 3 matter most to you? * Fixing local roads, pavements and cycle tracks Improving public transport Dealing with crime and antisocial behaviour Culture, leisure and sports facilities like youth clubs, libraries or Community centres Playgrounds and parks Activities for teenagers Local health services More homes for local people Skills, education and training opportunities for local people Childcare for local children, training and education opportunities, support for children with special educational needs How would you like to see the £20m spent? * What 2 things would you like to see more of? * Street Cleaning & Rubbish pick up Improving access to & repairing paths Community events for Young People Murals and Art Community Events for 65+ Sign cleaning Community Events for 25 – 65 Are there any local organisations / groups / businesses you would like to be represented on the community board? organisations most I will be in regular contact with people of Penhill and communicating through my social media channels to keep you updated on progress and get your feedback every step of the way. We also need local people to be members of Pride in Penhill’s community board, which will be responsible for listening to local feedback and planning how the community’s £20 million is spent. Do you want to receive updates from Will Stone MP on the progress of the Pride in Place Programme (Opt out at any time by sending your name and address to Will.stone.mp@parliament.uk) * — Select Choice — Yes No Do you want to be contacted about volunteering for the community board? * — Select Choice — Yes No Submit Pinetrees Community Centre, The Circle, Swindon, SN2 1RF 01793 540040 will@willstone.org.uk Links Standards Policy Privacy Policy Socials Facebook LinkedIn Instagram Bluesky YouTube TikTok © 2025 Will Stone MP
